roles.name: Medic
roles.description: Heals injured survivors. Cannot heal themselves — only another Medic can heal a Medic.

roles.name: Medic
roles.description: Heals injured survivors. Cannot heal themselves — only another Medic can heal a Medic.

roles.name: Scavenger
roles.description: Gathers food and resources from shrubs between disasters. Can share food with others.

roles.name: Scavenger
roles.description: Gathers food and resources from shrubs between disasters. Can share food with others.

roles.name: Engineer
roles.description: Must initialize construction. Without the Engineer, Constructors cannot begin repairs.

roles.name: Constructor
roles.description: Repairs shelter and furniture. Cannot begin until Engineer activates construction. Takes ~20 seconds.

roles.name: Constructor
roles.description: Repairs shelter and furniture. Cannot begin until Engineer activates construction. Takes ~20 seconds.

roles.name: Lookout
roles.description: Detects incoming disasters early, giving the group extra warning time.

roles.name: Lookout
roles.description: Detects incoming disasters early, giving the group extra warning time.

roles.name: Housekeeper
roles.description: Fixes the furniture for everyone to hide into. Restores furniture durability after events.

roles.description: Fixes the furniture for everyone to hide into. Restores furniture durability after events.

items.name: Repair Kit
items.description: Repairs 20 shelter integrity or one piece of furniture.

events.name: Earthquake
events.hint: The ground is shaking! Everyone brace and hang on!

events.name: Typhoon
events.hint: Fierce winds and flying debris are battering the house!

events.name: Meteor Shower
events.hint: Meteors are falling from the sky — take cover!

quizQuestions.q: What is RA 10121?
options: Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act

quizQuestions.q: What is Disaster Risk?
options: The probability of a disaster occurring
The damage caused by a disaster

quizQuestions.q: What is the difference between a hazard and a disaster?
options: A hazard is a potential threat; a disaster is when it causes damage
A hazard only refers to earthquakes

quizQuestions.q: What does 'resilience' mean in DRRR?
options: The ability to recover from disasters

quizQuestions.q: Which is an example of a natural hazard?
What is the first step in disaster preparedness?
What is an evacuation plan?
options: A strategy for safely leaving a danger zone
A plan for rebuilding after a disaster

quizQuestions.q: What is a 'safe room' designed for?
What does an early warning system do?
options: Alerts people before a disaster strikes

quizQuestions.q: Which is a man-made hazard?
What is the purpose of a first aid kit?
What is 'search and rescue'?
options: Finding and helping people trapped in disasters

quizQuestions.q: What should you do during an earthquake?
What is community-based disaster risk reduction?
options: Involving local communities in disaster preparedness

quizQuestions.q: What is a typhoon's 'eye'?
options: The calm center of the storm
The area where it makes landfall

quizQuestions.q: What does 'mitigation' mean in disaster management?
What is the Ring of Fire?
options: A zone of frequent earthquakes and volcanoes around the Pacific Ocean

quizQuestions.q: What is flood insurance?
What is a 'disaster drill'?
options: A practice exercise for emergency response
A tool for measuring earthquake intensity

quizQuestions.q: What role do volunteers play in DRRR?
options: They assist in preparedness, response, and recovery efforts
They are only needed for fundraising
They have no role in DRRR

quizQuestions.q: What is seismic intensity?
options: A measure of earthquake effects at a specific location

quizQuestions.q: What is a 'shelter-in-place' strategy?
options: Staying inside a sturdy building during a disaster

essayQuestions.q: A typhoon is approaching your community. You have 2 hours to prepare. What are the top 3 actions you would take and why?
After an earthquake, your neighbor is trapped under debris. Describe the steps you would take to help them safely.
Your school is in a flood-prone area. Propose a simple disaster preparedness plan for students.
Explain how a community early warning system can save lives during a volcanic eruption.
A classmate says 'Disasters only happen to other people.' How would you respond to change their mindset?
Describe the difference between being prepared and being paranoid about disasters.
If you could design a safe house, what features would it have and why?
How can technology help reduce disaster risks in your community?
